name: Route of the Serralada Marina
region_intro: Hiking trails around Santa Coloma De Gramenet are primarily found within the Parc de la Serralada de Marina, a mountainous area offering a natural escape from urban life. The region features diverse landscapes, including varied forests of cork oak, holm oak, and oak, alongside the rehabilitated banks of the River Besòs. Puig Castellar, at 299 meters, stands as a significant landmark, providing panoramic views of the surrounding area. Best hiking trails around Santa Coloma De Gramenet The most popular hiking route is Font de l'Alzina, Puig Castellar, Ermita de Sant Climent – Circular Route, a 5.3 miles (8.5 km) trail that takes 2 hours 41 minutes to complete. This moderate route includes the prominent Puig Castellar and the historic Ermita de Sant Climent. Another top favourite among local hikers is Puig Castellar, Ermita de Sant Climent, Cueva d'en Genís, Sant Jeroni de la Murtra – Circular Route, a moderate 5.2 miles (8.3 km) path. This trail explores the natural park, passing by the Cueva d'en Genís and offering views from Puig Castellar. Local hikers also love the Cavall Bernat y Puig Castellar — circular route, a 8.1 miles (13.1 km) trail leading through mountainous terrain and diverse forests, often completed in about 4 hours 15 minutes. Hiking around Santa Coloma De Gramenet is defined by mountainous terrain, diverse forests, and river banks. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to more challenging hikes. The routes in Santa Coloma De Gramenet are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 700 reviews. More than 7000 hikers have used komoot to explore Santa Coloma De Gramenet's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Santa Coloma De Gramenet?
A: There are over 50 hiking trails around Santa Coloma De Gramenet, primarily located within the Parc de la Serralada de Marina. These routes offer a range of difficulties, from easy walks to more challenging hikes.

Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, Santa Coloma De Gramenet offers 12 easy hiking trails. While specific easy routes aren't detailed here, the region's network includes options for different ability levels, making it accessible for families and beginners. Many trails within the Parc de la Serralada de Marina are well-signposted and some are adapted for individuals with mobility or visual disabilities.

Q: What kind of views can I expect on hikes in Santa Coloma De Gramenet?
A: Hikers can expect panoramic views, especially from higher points like Puig Castellar. From here, you can see Barcelona, Collserola, Montserrat, and Montseny. The trails also lead through diverse forested areas, including cork oak, holm oak, and oak forests, offering scenic beauty.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in the area?
A: Yes, several popular routes are circular. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e950386427/font-de-l-alzina-puig-castellar-ermita-de-sant-climent-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Font de l'Alzina, Puig Castellar, Ermita de Sant Climent – Circular Route&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 8.5 km trail. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e951644653/puig-castellar-ermita-de-sant-climent-cueva-d-en-genis-sant-jeroni-de-la-murtra-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puig Castellar, Ermita de Sant Climent, Cueva d'en Genís, Sant Jeroni de la Murtra – Circular Route&lt;/a&gt;, which is 8.3 km and explores the natural park.

Q: What historical or archaeological sites can I visit while hiking?
A: The region is rich in history. At the summit of Puig Castellar, you can explore the remains of an ancient &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/427891" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puig Castellar Iberian Settlement&lt;/a&gt;, dating back to the 6th to 2nd centuries BC. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e951644653/puig-castellar-ermita-de-sant-climent-cueva-d-en-genis-sant-jeroni-de-la-murtra-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puig Castellar, Ermita de Sant Climent, Cueva d'en Genís, Sant Jeroni de la Murtra – Circular Route&lt;/a&gt; passes by the Cueva d'en Genís, a notable paradolmen.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Santa Coloma De Gramenet?
A: The Parc de la Serralada de Marina offers pleasant hiking conditions for much of the year. Spring and autumn generally provide the most comfortable temperatures for exploring the trails and enjoying the diverse flora. While winter hiking is possible, it's always advisable to check local weather conditions.

Q: Are dogs allowed on the hiking trails?
A: The Parc de la Serralada de Marina, where most trails are located, generally permits dogs. However, it's always recommended to keep them on a leash, especially in protected areas or near wildlife, and to respect local regulations regarding pet access.

Q: How can I access the hiking trails using public transport?
A: Santa Coloma de Gramenet is well-connected to Barcelona by public transport, including metro and bus services. Many trailheads within the Parc de la Serralada de Marina are accessible from various points within the municipality, making it convenient to reach them without a car. Specific bus routes often serve areas bordering the park.

Q: What are some notable natural features to look out for on the trails?
A: Beyond the diverse forests, hikers can explore the Cueva d'en Genís, a unique paradolmen. The River Besòs also offers rehabilitated banks with paths for leisure. The mountainous terrain of the Parc de la Serralada de Marina itself is a significant natural feature, providing a green escape from urban life.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Santa Coloma De Gramenet?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 700 reviews. Reviewers often praise the diverse landscapes, the panoramic views from points like Puig Castellar, and the accessibility of a natural escape so close to the city.

Q: Are there any longer, more challenging hikes available?
A: Yes, for those seeking a longer challenge, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1328610914/cavall-bernat-y-puig-castellar-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cavall Bernat y Puig Castellar — circular route&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 13.1 km trail that typically takes over 4 hours to complete, leading through mountainous terrain and diverse forests.
tips: The hermitage of San Onofre is a small oratory located in the Badalonese neighborhood of Canyet built in 1498 by the monks of San Jerónimo de la Murtra on the hill of the Hermitages, at the top of the San Onofre hill (269 m.), between the Fuente Santa stream and the Coll de las Ermitas. Together with the hill of San Clemente it forms the Serrat de las Ermitas. It can be reached by the path that leaves the Coll de Vallençana or what passes through the Coll de Puig Castellar, from Santa Coloma de Gramenet.

The Sant Jeroni de la Murtra monastery is an old monastery of the Jerónima Order built in the Gothic style at the beginning of the 15th century. It is located in the natural environment of Serralada de la Marina, in the municipality of Badalona in the Province of Barcelona.
It is the aqueduct of Sant Jeroni de la Murtra. a well-preserved stretch of the already obsolete waterway that in a length of 37 km carried water to the Ciudad Condal.

Ca l'Alemany is an old farmhouse in the Canyet de Badalona neighborhood, located in the Betlem or San Jeroni valley, near the Turó de Montigalà, halfway between the city, marked by the B-20, and the Sant Jeroni de la Murtra monastery.

   Currently only ruins remain, the best preserved part of which are the laundry room in the courtyard and the arches that accompany it. Despite its status, it is currently protected as a Cultural Asset of Local Interest.

   An archaeological excavation confirmed the presence of a previous Roman villa, which would later have given way to the farmhouse. Despite its state, it is still a characteristic element of the landscape of the Sant Jeroni valley.
